1.3.0 (2026-05-01): apps axis 2nd domain hexa-filter-algebra registered (filter authoring/composition framework; sister to camera-filter-app — FILTER-ALGEBRA AUTHORS / COMPILES filters, camera-filter-app APPLIES them; both share 16.67 ms real-time budget; first intra-axis sister-pairing in n6-architecture). 9 primitive operations (M color matrix / T tone curve / K convolution / C color-space change / N grain / H histogram match / L local tone / V vignette / U sharpening) closed under composition algebra (∘ compose / ^ power / / scale / blend); auto-generation from N=5 reference image pairs (linear regression on 9 RGB unknowns + 1D regression on tone curve + FFT grain match + He 2015 residual CNN); LPIPS ≤ 0.15 / SSIM ≥ 0.95 / PSNR ≥ 35 dB provable quality bounds; 16.67 ms hard real-time ceiling at depth ≤ 4 (4 × 3.5 ms primitive avg = 14.0 ms ≤ 16.67 ms; depth 5 = 17.5 ms falsifier-trigger by construction). Design-by-physics per own#32, NOT n=6 force-fit. _total 385 -> 386 (+1); apps axis count 1 -> 2; alien-grade 10 PHYSICAL-LIMIT.

1.7.0 (2026-05-01): South Africa applied-tech bet #5 — biochar-dryland-restoration registered under life axis at alien-grade 10 PHYSICAL-LIMIT. Pyrolyze invasive Prosopis glandulosa + Acacia mearnsii (Australian wattle) + black wattle biomass into biochar at 5-20 t/ha amendment; stack Working-for-Water clearance subsidy USD 20-50/ha (DEFF 2023) + 10-25% Karoo rangeland carrying-capacity uplift (Hoffmann 2014 6-12 ha/LSU baseline) + Verra VM0044 / Puro durable-removal credits at USD 80-150/tCO2e. Physical-limit anchors: Antal-Grønli 2003 slow-pyrolysis char yield 25-35% biomass mass + 50-65% biomass-C retention (IECR 42:1619) · Lehmann 2007 / Singh 2012 Arrhenius mineralization E_a 75-120 kJ/mol → 100-1000 yr soil residence at 25 °C (GCB 18:2659) · Glaser-Lehmann 2002 CEC mass-balance mixing rule (BFS 35:219; Liang 2006 SSSAJ 70:1719) · Atkinson 2010 plant-available water uplift 5-15% per 10 t/ha (Plant Soil 337:1) · Smith-Bondeau 2014 SOC sink ~ 1.5 GtCO2/yr global (SA share ~ 36-50 MtCO2/yr; GCB 20:3270) · Verra VM0044 (2023) ≥ 100 yr + 10% buffer / Puro.earth Biochar v3 (2024) ≥ 1000 yr · Spokas 2010 H/C < 0.4 stability indicator · OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1000 CO 50 ppm + HCN 10 ppm 8-hr TWA. 6 precursors life/agriculture (Karoo carrying capacity Hoffmann 2014) + life/ecology (invasive ecology van Wilgen 2012 Biol Conserv 148:28) + life/herbalism (Acacia mearnsii bark 35-45% tannin Pizzi 1994) + materials/recycling (waste-stream conversion + EBC II PAH < 12 mg/kg) + physics/thermodynamics (Antal-Grønli + Arrhenius coupling) + materials/concrete-technology (Gupta 2018 1-5% cement substitution at parity strength). F-BIOCHAR-MVP-1..5 90-day MVP gates: F-MVP-4 (2026-09-30 carbon-price floor < USD 50/tCO2e) + F-MVP-1 + F-MVP-5 (2026-12-31 pilot kiln yield < 22% / OSHA PEL exceedance) + F-MVP-3 (2027-03-31 N=10 farm-pair carrying-cap uplift < 8%) + F-MVP-2 (2027-06-30 5-yr soil residence < 80%). Hardest unknown per proposals/south-africa-applied-tech.md: voluntary carbon market integrity durability (Verra integrity scandals 2023-2024); contingency = SA Carbon Tax 2019 R190/tCO2e + Article 6.4 ITMOs + concrete-technology cross-utilization. Design-by-physics per own#32, NOT n=6 force-fit. Lint: own#31 v3.19 PASS; own_doc_lint --rule 6 + 15 PASS. _total 392 -> 393 (+1); life axis count 47 -> 48; alien-grade 10 PHYSICAL-LIMIT.

2.0.0 (2026-05-01): South Africa applied-tech bet #2 — crispr-cas13-poc-diagnostic registered under life axis at alien-grade 10 PHYSICAL-LIMIT (proposals/south-africa-applied-tech.md row 2). Field-deployable CRISPR-Cas13 isothermal SHERLOCK/DETECTR diagnostic for TB + HIV with lateral-flow Au-NP readout, 30-60 min TTR, lyophilized reagents, USD 2-5/test + USD 200-500 reader. Targets World #3 TB burden 450,000 cases/yr South Africa + 13% HIV prevalence 8.2M PLHIV vs current lab-bound NAATs (GeneXpert MTB/RIF USD 10-15/cartridge + USD 17,000 instrument requiring 4°C cold chain + AC mains). Physical-limit anchors: Abudayyeh-Zhang 2017 (Nature 550:280) Cas13 trans-cleavage k_cat 30-50 /s/molecule + Eigen-Hammes 1963 universal diffusion-limit ceiling k_cat/K_M ≤ 10⁹ M⁻¹s⁻¹ + Mason-Botella 2020 (Anal. Chem. 92:14644) lyophilized-reagent Arrhenius E_a ≈ 80 kJ/mol → 12 mo @ 25°C / 60% RH (no cold chain) + Posthuma-Trumpie 2009 (Anal. Bioanal. Chem. 393:569) lateral-flow Au-NP visual LOD ~10 fM analyte / ~10⁵ copies viral RNA + Piepenburg-Armes 2006 (PLOS Biol. 4:e204) RPA isothermal pre-amplification 10⁹ in 20 min at 37-42°C. Clinical anchors: WHO Global TB Report 2023 + WHO TB TPP 2014 (≤ 10³ CFU/mL PoC class) + WHO HIV-VL 2021 (1,000 cp/mL virological-failure threshold) + Thierry 1990 IS6110 multi-copy 10-15 / Mtb cell + UNAIDS 2023 SA 8.2M PLHIV. 6 precursors life/biology-medical (TB/HIV pathology) + life/synbio (LbuCas13a engineering) + life/genetics (28-nt crRNA + RPA primers) + life/hiv-treatment (WHO VL clinical thresholds) + materials/ceramics (Whatman FF120HP nitrocellulose) + physics/optics (Mie 1908 / Haiss 2007 Au-NP plasmon). F-CAS13-MVP-1..5 falsifier gates: F-MVP-1 + F-MVP-3 (2026-09-30 lyo activity < 50% after 12-mo @ 25°C / HIV LOD > 1,000 cp/mL) + F-MVP-2 (2026-10-31 TB sensitivity < 90% smear-pos) + F-MVP-5 (2026-12-31 cost > USD 8 at 100k-test/yr) + F-MVP-4 (2027-03-31 SAHPRA Class C IVD timeline > 24 mo). Hardest unknown per SA portfolio: lyophilized-reagent shelf life under field humidity 60-90% RH (gated by F-CAS13-MVP-1 ICH Q1A R2 accelerated study). Design-by-physics per own#32, NOT n=6 force-fit. 2.1.0 (2026-05-01): South Africa applied-tech bet #3 — amd-ree-mineshaft-phes registered under energy axis at alien-grade 10 PHYSICAL-LIMIT (proposals/south-africa-applied-tech.md row 3). Witwatersrand/Mpumalanga acid-mine drainage (AMD) treated as REE/base-metal precipitate-mining feedstock + decommissioned 1-3 km mine shafts repurposed as Pumped Hydroelectric Energy Storage (PHES) lower reservoirs. Physical-limit anchors: Bernoulli 1738 specific potential energy E = ρ·g·h = 2.72 kWh/m³ at 1 km head (NIST CODATA g=9.80665 m/s²; cross-check IEA 2021 PHES handbook quote 2.7 kWh/m³ per 1 km head) · IEA 2021 PHES Handbook modern round-trip efficiency 80-87% with reversible-pump-turbine ceiling ~ 0.95 (Goldisthal 0.872 observed per Engle 2018; mk1 design 0.82) · Baes & Mesmer 1976 REE-hydroxide solubility products Y(OH)₃ K_sp 10⁻²² + Eu(OH)₃ 10⁻²⁴ + Fe(OH)₃ 4×10⁻³⁸ + Al(OH)₃ 3×10⁻³⁴ + Byrne 1988 / Bau 1999 Fe-Al hydroxide scavenger window pH 5-6 + Verplanck 2004 USGS / Ayora 2016 70-95% REE coprecipitation yield · Sastri-Shibata 2003 D2EHPA solvent-extraction selectivity β_Y/Eu 1.5-3.0 (mk1 design 2.5) + Kremser 1930 5-stage countercurrent for 99% recovery · McCarthy 2010 Witwatersrand basin geology (pH 2-4 + Fe 100-1000 mg/L + sulfate 1000-5000 mg/L + REE 10-200 ppb per Naicker 2003 / Olias 2005) + Singer-Stumm 1970 pyrite-oxidation pH chemistry · ASTM C150 Type V sulfate-resistant Portland cement 28 MPa compressive at 28 d vs 9.81 MPa hydrostatic at 1 km depth (2.85× margin) · NREL 2020 PHES grid-services < 30 s primary-frequency-response (Eskom 50 Hz tie) · Lazard LCOS v8 2022 PHES vs battery LCOS comparison (PHES wins long-duration > 6 hr at USD 50-150/MWh) · Schreiber 2021 J Cleaner Production REE recycling LCA (30-50% of primary-mining energy intensity). 6 precursors energy/power-grid + energy/battery-architecture + materials/recycling + physics/fluid + physics/thermodynamics + materials/concrete-technology. Pilot scale 1000 m³/day AMD + 100,000 m³ reservoir / 1 km head / 60 MW pump-turbine = 272 MWh raw / 223 MWh round-trip + ~ 25.5 kg/yr REE oxide. F-AMD-MVP-1..5 falsifier gates: F-MVP-3 (2026-09-30 PHES RTE < 75% in pilot) + F-MVP-1 + F-MVP-2 + F-MVP-5 (2026-12-31 REE yield < 60% / shaft integrity < 25 yr cyclic-PHES design life / SA DMR licensing > 36 months) + F-MVP-4 (2027-06-30 REE basket > 50% drop from 2024 baseline + IRR < 8%). Hardest unknown per proposals row 3: REE basket price volatility ±30-50%; mitigation = dual revenue (REE + storage) where storage alone may carry IRR. Design-by-physics per own#32, NOT n=6 force-fit. Hardest unknown per proposals row 6: device-attrition rate (5% sustains, 25% holds ceiling, 30% destroys unit economics); central estimate 10-20% based on Boox Palma 2 retail ~10% + Equip Africa 2023 SA-rural tablet 14%. 2.3.0 (2026-05-01): South Africa applied-tech bet #1 — rooftop-pv-2nd-life-microgrid registered under energy axis at alien-grade 10 PHYSICAL-LIMIT (proposals/south-africa-applied-tech.md row 1). 50-500 kW rooftop PV + 100-500 kWh second-life NMC EV-battery microgrid for SA clinic/school/SME anchor sites; reference T2 100 kW PV + 250 kWh battery at USD 150,000 capex with 6.7-yr simple payback at SA loadshedding-blended avoided-cost tariff 0.17 USD/kWh (Eskom 0.11 base × 75% + diesel-genset 0.35 × 25% loadshedding share); LCOE 0.12 USD/kWh; discounted payback 11 yr at r=8%. Physical-limit anchors: Shockley-Queisser 1961 PV ceiling 33.7% at Eg=1.34 eV (commercial Si 22% = 65% of SQ peak; Tiedje 1984 + Ruhle 2016) + NREL TMY3 / SAURAN 2400 kWh/m^2/yr SA GHI (top decile globally; PR 0.80 → 1920 kWh/kW/yr) + Wood-Mackenzie 2023 NMC 2nd-life 1500-2500 cycles to 60% SOH at 80% DoD (10-yr calendar life; Schmalstieg 2014 + Wang 2014 cycle-life model; eta_RT 0.90; lifetime 972 kWh/kWh nameplate) + Spotnitz-Franklin 2003 Li-ion thermal runaway 80°C onset (Bernardi 1985 heat-gen + Incropera 2017 nat-conv h=7.5 W/m^2/K → 38°C steady-state runaway margin at 40°C SA design ambient with extended-fin radiator 32 m^2/250 kWh) + Esram-Chapman 2007 MPPT 99% theoretical × 96% inverter (SMA Sunny Tripower spec) + Cole 1990 LCOE financial-physics. 6 precursors energy/solar-architecture (SQ ceiling + insolation) + energy/battery-architecture (NMC electrochemistry + cycle life) + energy/power-grid (grid-tie inverter + microgrid topology + NRS-097-2/NERSA SSEG) + energy/thermal-management (passive cooling SA peak ambient) + physics/electromagnetism (semiconductor band gap) + materials/recycling (2nd-life EV pack reclamation; Nissan Leaf / Tesla Model S retired packs 70-80% SOH; BloombergNEF 2024 1 GWh/yr supply). F-PV2L-MVP-1..5 90-day MVP gates: F-PV2L-MVP-1 + F-PV2L-MVP-3 + F-PV2L-MVP-5 (2026-09-30 cycle-life < 1500 / SCR < 70% / thermal management > 35°C internal at SA peak summer) + F-PV2L-MVP-2 (2026-10-31 inverter MTBF < 10 yr in SA dust+heat) + F-PV2L-MVP-4 (2026-12-31 NERSA SSEG approval > 18 months). Hardest unknown per SA portfolio row 1: 2nd-life cycle-life data scarcity (Wood-Mackenzie 2023 industry data converges on 2000-cycle midpoint but SA-pilot validation needed). Design-by-physics per own#32, NOT n=6 force-fit.
